intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant 'Angorkis infundubularis'. It's possible that this plant does not exist or is not well-documented. However, if you are referring to the plant 'Andrographis paniculata', also known as 'Kalmegh' or 'King of Bitters', I can provide you with the following information:

Andrographis paniculata is a plant species in the family Acanthaceae, commonly used in traditional medicine in Asia. The health benefits of Andrographis paniculata include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include andrographolide, a diterpenoid lactone with anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Andrographis paniculata include teas, capsules, and tablets, as well as extracts and tinctures used in traditional medicine.
